pack.title: Core Pack
pack.description: Thirty starter challenges for Typ-Nique spanning notation, layout, formatting, and mixed Typst fluency.

challenges.title: Arithmetic Sprint
challenges.hint: This one is just clean inline math.
challenges.explanation: A minimal warmup challenge to introduce the basic math syntax and spacing style.

challenges.title: Order of Ops
challenges.hint: Use parentheses exactly once.
challenges.explanation: A straightforward expression that checks grouping and clean multiplication syntax.

challenges.title: Quick Difference
challenges.hint: Don’t overthink it: plain subtraction with a negative result.
challenges.explanation: Good for reinforcing that Typst math input stays pleasantly uncluttered for simple expressions.

challenges.title: Root Check
challenges.hint: Use the function form for square root.
challenges.explanation: Introduces a function-style math construct while staying short enough for a competitive round.

challenges.title: Half Step
challenges.hint: Just a simple slash fraction.
challenges.explanation: A tiny fraction challenge that helps players settle into Typst’s math rhythm.

challenges.title: Balanced Ratio
challenges.hint: Numerator is grouped.
challenges.explanation: Tests grouping inside a fraction while still keeping the expression readable at game speed.

challenges.title: Tower Fraction
challenges.hint: Both numerator and denominator are grouped.
challenges.explanation: A classic rational expression that feels familiar but still punishes dropped parentheses.

challenges.title: Three Layer Sip
challenges.hint: It’s a quotient of two grouped expressions, each with a tiny fraction inside.
challenges.explanation: A great competitive prompt because the syntax is compact but the nesting is easy to stumble on.

challenges.title: Square It
challenges.hint: Just one superscript.
challenges.explanation: Tiny but useful for teaching the caret syntax that shows up everywhere later.

challenges.title: Indexed Sequence
challenges.hint: Three comma-separated subscripts.
challenges.explanation: A fast sequence prompt that feels satisfying when typed cleanly.

challenges.title: Power Stack
challenges.hint: Both variables have the same index and power.
challenges.explanation: Combines subscript and superscript syntax in a compact, race-friendly expression.

challenges.title: Limit Marker
challenges.hint: The second subscript needs grouping.
challenges.explanation: This challenge feels simple visually, but it rewards players who know when grouped subscripts matter.

challenges.title: Mini Identity
challenges.hint: Use `mat` with semicolons for rows.
challenges.explanation: Introduces Typst matrix syntax with the friendliest possible example: the 2x2 identity.

challenges.title: Swap Matrix
challenges.hint: Same syntax as the identity, different entries.
challenges.explanation: A quick matrix variation that feels easy to read but still tests row separators under pressure.

challenges.title: Triangular Trouble
challenges.hint: A 3x3 matrix with semicolons between rows.
challenges.explanation: The first serious matrix challenge in the pack. It’s highly legible but easy to mistype mid-race.

challenges.title: Pythagorean Theorem
challenges.hint: The classic right-triangle identity.
challenges.explanation: Replaces the layout-only prompt with one of the most recognizable equations in mathematics.

challenges.title: Euler Identity
challenges.hint: Use a grouped exponent on e.
challenges.explanation: A famous compact identity linking e, i, pi, 1, and 0.

challenges.title: Mass-Energy Equivalence
challenges.hint: Only one variable has a superscript.
challenges.explanation: Swaps a non-math layout drill for Einstein's iconic equation.

challenges.title: Newton Second Law
challenges.hint: Short, clean, and purely symbolic.
challenges.explanation: Keeps this slot mathematical with a foundational mechanics equation.

challenges.title: Infinity Loop
challenges.hint: Use the named symbol.
challenges.explanation: A quick symbol recall challenge that rewards Typst vocabulary rather than keyboard gymnastics.

challenges.title: Not Equal
challenges.hint: Typst has a clean ASCII operator here.
challenges.explanation: Shows that some polished math output comes from pleasantly simple source syntax.

challenges.title: Arrow Run
challenges.hint: It uses two different arrow operators.
challenges.explanation: A nice race challenge because the visual output is simple but the exact operators matter.

challenges.title: Subset Signal
challenges.hint: Use a named set-theory relation.
challenges.explanation: This one feels clever because the rendered symbol is obvious, but remembering the Typst name is the real test.

challenges.title: Circle Area
challenges.hint: Area equals pi times radius squared.
challenges.explanation: Replaces text formatting content with a standard geometric formula.

challenges.title: Circle Circumference
challenges.hint: Start with C on the left and a 2 on the right.
challenges.explanation: Another familiar formula that keeps the pack focused on math-only prompts.

challenges.title: Quadratic Root
challenges.hint: The numerator uses -b plus a square root.
challenges.explanation: Introduces a famous algebra formula in a single-root form that stays Typst-friendly.

challenges.title: Sphere Volume
challenges.hint: Volume equals four-thirds pi r cubed.
challenges.explanation: Replaces the small text label with a well-known volume formula.

challenges.title: Styled Equation
challenges.hint: It’s centered with a block wrapper around inline math.
challenges.explanation: A satisfying mixed prompt because it combines layout structure with familiar symbolic content.

challenges.title: Difference Of Squares
challenges.hint: Factor the left side into two parentheses.
challenges.explanation: Removes the text label wrapper and replaces it with a classic algebra identity.

challenges.title: Distance Formula
challenges.hint: Both coordinate differences are squared inside one square root.
challenges.explanation: Keeps the mixed slot mathematical by switching to a famous coordinate-geometry formula.
